LIVING WITH

LIVING WITH YOUR IBD,

BEFORE LEAVING :

= you must be up to date with DT Polio vaccinations ;

æ if you are on anti-TNF therapy, the pneumococcal vaccine is recommended: Prevenar 13 then Pneurmo 23 with an 8‑week interval ;

for the yellow fever vaccine :
- it is contraindicated if you are taking an anti-TNF treatment, an immunosuppressant (such as Imurel or others), or corticosteroids at more than 20 mg/day
- if the vaccine is essential you must stop the treatment 3 months before the vaccine until 3 weeks after ;

for malaria: you should take the same treatments as people who do not have health problems. The treatment depends on the country or even the region of destination; you should get information from travel advice services, which generally exist in university hospitals (CHU).

#04

IBD AND TRAVEL

Everyone dreams of distant horizons synonymous with discovery and holidays...
This fact sheet offers you an exhaustive list - "to do list" - aimed at giving yourself every means to "keep a good distance from your IBD" during your stay abroad.
